DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Rottinghaus, Esq. and Jacob Moss, Esq. (925-838-2090) of Berding & Weil LLP for Plaintiff.
Ashton McKinnon, Esq. (818-710-7777 ext 244) of Zimmerman & Kahanowitch for Defendant CJUF III 1998 PROPERTY LLC, CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC through court Call.
Benjamin Sanchez, Esq. (925-222-3400) of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendant Bay city Mechanical, Inc. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling. Defendant's Counsel for CJUF III 1998 argues CC& R's. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CJUF III 1998 PROPERTY LLC, CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendants CJUF III 1998 Property LLC and Cahill Contractor's, Inc.'s demurrer to the second and fourth cause of action for enforcement of government documents and breach of construction contracts in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Linea Owners Association is overruled as to both causes of action. Linea has adequately alleged the legal effect of the contracts it contends were breached by CJUF and Cahill. At least in some circumstances CC&Rs constitute an enforceable contract between the developer and an owners association (Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. (2012) 55 Cal. 4th 223) and thus the allegations that the CC&Rs in this case constitute a contract that Linea can sue CJUF for breach withstand a demurrer. Linea alleges sufficient facts that it was an intended third party beneficiary of the construction contracts. Those facts are that from the inception of the project's development, CJUF intended to create a homeowner association to manage the property and the contracting parties were aware that the association would exist and benefit from their contracts. Thus, even though the contractual terms do not make the parties' intent evident, it was known that their contractual performance was for the benefit of the association. These facts make the fourth cause of action more like Gilbert Financial Corp. (1978) 82 Cal. App. 3d 65 than Landale-Cameron Court, Inc. v. Ahonen (2007) 155 Cal. App. 4th 1401). (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Rottinghaus, Esq. and Jacob Moss, Esq. (925-838-2090) of Berding & Weil LLP for Plaintiff.
Ashton McKinnon, Esq. (818-710-7777 ext 244) of Zimmerman & Kahanowitch for Defendant CJUF III 1998 PROPERTY LLC, CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC through court Call.
Benjamin Sanchez, Esq. (925-222-3400) of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendant Bay city Mechanical, Inc. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling. Defendant's Counsel for CJUF III 1998 argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Counsel for Plaintiff indicates and clarifies there are no allegations seeking attorney's fees.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with addition as follows:

DEFENDANT CJUF III 1998 PROPERTY LLC, CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendants CJUF III 1998 Property LLC and Cahill Contractors, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Linea Owners Association is denied in its entirety. None of the allegations that are sought to be stricken are improper, even if they do not, by themselves, support any relief for which Linea seek. The attorney's fees allegations are not improper because CCP 5975(c) provides a statutory basis for recovery of fees.

In addition, Plaintiff may not seek attorney's fees solely for breach of contract based on the Davis Sterling act. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)
